title: cinder component constraints
description: Per-component discriminated-union usage rules that cannot be cleanly expressed in JSON Schema. Each rule encodes a cross-prop constraint using a small four-combinator DSL. The reference evaluator in the cinder package checks props at runtime and returns violations.

$schema.description: Relative path to the JSON Schema that validates this file.

component.description: Kebab-case id of the component these constraints apply to. Must match a component id in components.json.

rules.description: Ordered list of constraint rules. All rules are evaluated independently; multiple rules may fire simultaneously.

Rule.description: A single constraint rule. The rule's 'of' predicate tree is evaluated against the component's rendered props. When the rule fires, the evaluator returns a violation with the rule's id, severity, and message derived from 'description'.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this rule. Referenced in violation reports and test fixtures.

severity.description: How severe a violation of this rule is. 'error' means the component will not render correctly or accessibly; 'warning' means a likely mistake; 'info' means a best-practice note.

kind.description: The logical combinator for the 'of' array. 'exactlyOne': exactly one predicate in 'of' must be satisfied. 'anyOf': at least one predicate in 'of' must be satisfied. 'allOf': all predicates in 'of' must be satisfied. 'requires': when the optional 'when' condition is satisfied, all predicates in 'of' must be satisfied.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the constraint, written as a positive statement of what must hold. Shown to developers in violation messages.

when.description: Optional pre-condition for 'requires' rules. When present, the rule only fires if this predicate is satisfied. Not used by 'exactlyOne', 'anyOf', or 'allOf' rules.

of.description: The predicate branches evaluated by the 'kind' combinator.

examples.description: Representative valid and invalid prop objects for documentation and testing. The evaluator test suite uses these as fixtures.

valid.description: Prop objects that satisfy this rule.

invalid.description: Prop objects that violate this rule.

Predicate.description: A leaf or composite predicate in the constraint DSL. Each predicate tests one aspect of the component's rendered props.

PropEqualsPredicate.description: True when the named prop is strictly equal to the given value.

equals.description: The value the prop must equal. May be any JSON-serializable scalar.

PropExistsPredicate.description: True when the named prop is present (not undefined or null).

prop.description: The prop name to test for presence.

exists.description: Must always be true; the predicate type is 'prop exists'.

PropNonEmptyPredicate.description: True when the named prop is a non-empty string.

prop.description: The prop name to test for a non-empty string value.

nonEmpty.description: Must always be true; the predicate type is 'prop non-empty'.

SnippetPredicate.description: True when the named Svelte snippet is passed (i.e. the slot/snippet prop is truthy).

snippet.description: The snippet (slot) prop name to test for presence.

AllOfPredicate.description: Composite predicate that is true when all nested predicates are satisfied.

allOf.description: All predicates in this array must be satisfied.

AnyOfPredicate.description: Composite predicate that is true when at least one nested predicate is satisfied.

anyOf.description: At least one predicate in this array must be satisfied.
